disclaimer: AEMF framing only — not a second realize engine. Aspects situate proven fault classes in air / electricity / mechanical / fluid media for operator comprehension and playbook selection. Playbook prose guides approach order; LOGOS still owns membership.

air.label: Air
air.summary: Intake, combustion air metering, EVAP, EGR, catalyst, and oxygen sensing that characterize gas-path health.
air.playbookGuidance: Prefer proving gas-path integrity with trims, O₂/Mode 06, and leak/purge checks before condemning unrelated electrics. Do not clear codes to “see if it comes back” while unburned fuel or lean/rich extremes remain active.

electricity.label: Electricity
electricity.summary: Circuits, coils, injectors, heaters, and sensors whose primary evidence is electrical integrity.
electricity.playbookGuidance: Prefer circuit, connector, and actuator electrical checks (power/ground/signal, coil/injector/heater tests) before invasive mechanical teardown. A circuit DTC is not yet a failed part — verify the wire path.

mechanical.label: Mechanical
mechanical.summary: Timing, correlation, and load-driven mechanical events proved from misfire / cam-crank evidence.
mechanical.playbookGuidance: Prefer isolation that can move or rule out a mechanical cause (swap test, compression/leak-down, correlation evidence) before replacing sensors that may only be reporting the consequence. Sustained mechanical misfire under load risks catalyst damage — stabilize first.

fluid.label: Fluid
fluid.summary: Oil, coolant, and electrohydraulic media that condition actuators and thermal control.
fluid.playbookGuidance: Prefer verifying level, quality, viscosity, and thermal media before blaming electrohydraulic or thermal actuators. Wrong or starved fluid often mimics actuator failure (especially MultiAir and thermostat-related complaints).

playbookNotes.MisfireUnderLoad: Order the playbook mechanical → air: isolate ignition/cylinder mechanical cause (swap, compression) before chasing intake or fuel-trim corrections. Unburned fuel is an air-path consequence that can destroy the catalyst if you keep driving.
playbookNotes.CamCrankCorrelationFault: Treat as mechanical timing/correlation first. Do not clear and drive hoping for a soft reset — correlation faults often precede valvetrain or chain damage.
playbookNotes.LeanFuelBank1: Stay on the air/fuel metering path: vacuum leaks, MAF/MAP plausibility, fuel delivery, then O₂ confirmation. Avoid random coil or injector swaps without trim/evidence alignment.
playbookNotes.LeanFuelBank2: Stay on the air/fuel metering path for bank 2: leaks, metering sensors, fuel delivery, then O₂ confirmation.
playbookNotes.RichFuelBank1: Stay on the air/fuel metering path: fuel pressure/injector contribution, purge influence, then O₂ confirmation. Rich under load also risks catalyst damage.
playbookNotes.RichFuelBank2: Stay on the air/fuel metering path for bank 2; confirm purge and injector contribution before sensor replacement.
playbookNotes.EvapLeakSmall: Smoke/leak and seal integrity first; purge/vent electrical only after the gas-path leak search is disciplined.
playbookNotes.EvapLeakLarge: Large EVAP leaks are air-path seal failures — find the open path before replacing solenoids.
playbookNotes.EvapPurgeSystemFault: Prove purge flow and commanded operation; distinguish stuck-open purge (rich/idle) from circuit faults.
playbookNotes.EvapVentSystemFault: Vent control is still an EVAP air-path function; verify vent path and electrical control together.
playbookNotes.CatalystEfficiencyBank1: Catalyst efficiency is an air-path outcome — rule out upstream misfire/fuel trim causes before condemning the converter.
playbookNotes.CatalystEfficiencyBank2: Same as bank 1: fix upstream air/fuel/misfire contributors before converter replacement.
playbookNotes.O2CircuitFaultBank1: Electricity first (heater/signal circuit), then interpret air-path readings only after the circuit is sound.
playbookNotes.O2CircuitFaultBank2: Electricity first on bank 2 circuit integrity; do not tune fuel from a broken sensor circuit.
playbookNotes.O2PerformanceFaultBank1: Sensor performance is air-path behavior once the circuit is healthy — confirm wiring/heater before replacing the sensor for “lazy” response.
playbookNotes.O2PerformanceFaultBank2: Confirm circuit health, then judge bank 2 sensor performance against Mode 06 / response evidence.
playbookNotes.O2HeaterFaultBank1: Heater faults are electrical — repair the heater circuit before trusting closed-loop fuel control.
playbookNotes.O2HeaterFaultBank2: Heater faults are electrical on bank 2; do not chase trim until the heater path works.
playbookNotes.O2DownstreamCircuitFaultBank1: Downstream circuit = electricity first; catalyst judgments wait on a sound sensor path.
playbookNotes.O2DownstreamCircuitFaultBank2: Downstream bank 2 circuit integrity before efficiency conclusions.
playbookNotes.O2DownstreamPerformanceFaultBank1: Downstream performance informs catalyst air-path health only after circuit/heater integrity.
playbookNotes.O2DownstreamPerformanceFaultBank2: Same discipline on bank 2 downstream performance.
playbookNotes.O2DownstreamHeaterFaultBank1: Electrical heater repair first; then re-evaluate catalyst monitors.
playbookNotes.O2DownstreamHeaterFaultBank2: Electrical heater repair first on bank 2 downstream.
playbookNotes.EgrFlowFault: EGR is an air-path flow problem — verify passages and commanded flow before sensor swaps.
playbookNotes.EgrCircuitFault: EGR circuit faults are electricity-led; prove the control/sensor circuit before cleaning passages alone.
playbookNotes.SecondaryAirSystemFault: Secondary air is an air-path emissions aid — verify pump/valve flow and control before unrelated ignition work.
playbookNotes.CoolantThermostatFault: Fluid/thermal control first: thermostat and coolant progress, not sensor replacement by default.
playbookNotes.EctSensorCircuitFault: Electricity on the ECT circuit first; only then trust temperature for fluid/thermal strategy.
playbookNotes.IgnitionCoilCircuitFault: Coil circuit is electricity — prove primary/secondary circuit before compression testing unless misfire evidence already demands it.
playbookNotes.InjectorCircuitFault: Injector circuit is electricity with a fluid delivery consequence — verify the electrical path before rail teardown.
playbookNotes.MapSensorFault: MAP/baro circuit is electricity reporting air density — verify the sensor circuit before chasing vacuum leaks blindly.
playbookNotes.KnockSensorCircuitFault: Knock circuit is electricity that protects mechanical integrity — repair the circuit; do not ignore knock protection.
playbookNotes.ThrottlePositionSensorFault: TPS circuit is electricity that shapes air metering — correlate A/B rationality before throttle body replacement.
playbookNotes.ChronicOilConsumption: Fluid loss with mechanical wear risk — trend oil/odometer honestly; do not treat as a one-scan DTC story.
playbookNotes.MultiAirOilStarvation: Fluid before actuator: level, quality, viscosity, and oil-related TSBs before condemning the MultiAir unit (see FCA guidance).
